taskDescription: Arrange the gluteal muscles and structures in order from superficial to deep.
paragraphs: Gluteus maximus — the largest and most superficial muscle, a powerful extensor and lateral rotator of the hip.
Gluteus medius — the primary hip abductor, responsible for preventing pelvic drop during walking (Trendelenburg sign when weak).
Gluteus minimus — the deepest of the three gluteal muscles, also an abductor and medial rotator.
The six deep lateral rotators (piriformis, obturator internus, gemelli, quadratus femoris) — piriformis is the key landmark for the sciatic nerve.
The sciatic nerve — the largest nerve in the body, usually exits below piriformis through the greater sciatic foramen.
